title: Stephen A Smith reveals he would vote for Marco Rubio over several Democrats for president
description: Commentator Stephen A Smith has shockingly revealed he would vote for Secretary of State Marco Rubio and ruled out running for president in 2028.  Smith’s remarks came during an appearance on the premiere of Fox News host Sean Hannity’s podcast, ‘Hang Out with Sean Hannity’.  During the interview, Smith revealed his top three candidates he would support in the 2028 presidential election.  Smith claimed he would vote for Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Pennsylvania Governor Josh Shapiro or Rubio.  When asked why he would support Rubio, Smith said, “He's an adult in the room. There is no questioning his qualifications for the job.”

title: Stock market in ‘positive territory’ despite ‘volatile’ Middle East situation
description: Sky News Business Reporter Edward Boyd has claimed the stock market is in “positive territory” for a second day in a row despite a “volatile” period amid the Middle East conflict.

title: Matt Canavan goes after Labor after winning Nationals leadership
description: Nationals Leader Matt Canavan speaks after winning the party leadership following David Littleproud’s shock resignation.  “I want to thank David Littleproud for his excellent service to our party, to our nation,” Mr Canavan said.  “The bad news is that things are not going well under this Labor government.”

title: Karoline Leavitt savagely demands Trump’s SAVE America Act to be passed
description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt has demanded on behalf of US President Donald Trump that the SAVE America Act be passed in the Senate.  The SAVE America Act passed Congress the first time, and it faces a roadblock in the Senate as it needs 60 votes to get through due to a filibuster rule.  Donald Trump wants to add amendments to the SAVE America Act, which will require the Bill to pass Congress a second time.  Donald Trump said on Truth Social earlier in the week that he "will not sign other Bills" until his SAVE America Act has been passed into legislation.  He said, “It must be done immediately. It supersedes everything else.”  “President, will not sign other Bills until this is passed, AND NOT THE WATERED DOWN VERSION.”

title: ‘Million miles away’: Barnaby Joyce calls out Coalition policy disunity
description: One Nation MP Barnaby Joyce claims new Nationals Leader Matt Canavan’s policy beliefs are a “million miles away” from much of the Liberal Party.  “I know Matt, I know him very well, and he is an exceptional guy,” Mr Joyce told Sky News Australia.  “His policy beliefs are just a million miles away from where a lot of the Liberal Party is.”

title: ‘They were hopeless’: Joyce applauds Canavan-led Nats following Littleproud resignation
description: One Nation MP Barnaby Joyce claims the Nationals were heading in a “hopeless” direction before making Matt Canavan leader.  “When you play first grade, you want to play up against other first graders,” Mr Joyce told Sky News Australia.  “Honestly, where they were going, it was just hopeless.”

title: ‘Starmer is letting this happen’: The IRGC has ‘deeply infiltrated Britain’
description: Iranian activist Sheyda Rahbari has alleged that the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has “deeply infiltrated” Britain.  “Starmer is letting this happen by not designating them as a terrorist; he’s allowing them to operate freely, and this must stop,” she told Sky News host James Bolt.  “You can’t negotiate with a mafia. The only way to deal with them is to dismantle them completely in Tehran and London.”

title: ‘He lives in luxury while Iranians are starving’: Mojtaba Khamenei ruthlessly exposed
description: Iranian activist Sheyda Rahbari claims the new Iranian supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei is “just like his father” except he’s a “bigger hypocrite”.  “While he says the West is the enemy, but he earns property empire in London worth hundreds of millions,” he told Sky News host James Bolt.  “He lives in luxury while Iranians are starving.”

title: ‘Out of touch’ Keir Starmer under fire for ‘hiding’ as Iran war rages on
description: Iranian activist Sheyda Rahbari has hit out at UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer and the woke left for being “out of touch” when it comes to the Iranian regime and the IRGC.  “The British people support freedom and understand the truth,” she told Sky News host James Bolt.  “But Starmer is hiding behind diplomacy via the IRGC, turns London into the playground.”

title: ‘We want them gone’: Barnaby Joyce blasts Chris Bowen, Climate Change and Energy Department
description: One Nation MP Barnaby Joyce claims One Nation wants the Climate Change and Energy Department to be gone.  “We want to get rid of the Climate Change Department,” Mr Joyce told Sky News Australia.  “We want the Climate Change and Energy Department gone, finished, and Mr Bowen with it.”

title: 'Looking forward to it’: Barnaby Joyce excited to debate Nats under Matt Canavan
description: One Nation MP Barnaby Joyce says he is looking forward to debating the National Party under Matt Canavan’s leadership.  “It’s going to be a lot more interesting,” Mr Joyce told Sky News Australia.  “I’m looking forward to it.”

title: Karoline Leavitt puts US troops wounded toll ‘in the ballpark’ of 150
description: White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt acknowledges that roughly 150 American service members have been wounded in the ongoing conflict with Iran, saying that figure is “in the ballpark” of the true number.  “I can’t confirm the exact number, I know it’s within that ballpark,” Ms Leavitt said.  “I would defer you to the Pentagon for a specific number of wounded or injured thus far.”

title: Karoline Leavitt obliterates The New York Times over Iranian school strike claims
description: White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt blasts The New York Times for “harassing” the Trump administration about the strikes on an Iranian girls' school.   “I know there's been a lot of speculation in the media about who may be responsible for this, we're not going to get ahead of the Department of War in the conclusion of that investigation,” Ms Leavitt said.  “Frankly, we're not going to be harassed by The New York Times, who's been putting out a lot of articles on this, making claims that have just not been verified by the Department of War.”

title: Iranian security chief issues chilling threat to ‘eliminate’ Donald Trump
description: A senior Iranian security official has issued a brutal threat against US President Donald Trump amid escalating tensions.  “The Ashura-loving nation of Iran does not fear your paper threats. Even those greater than you could not eliminate the Iranian nation,” Supreme National Security Council Secretary Ali Larijani said.  “Watch out for yourself — lest you be eliminated!”  The remark came after Trump vowed devastating retaliation if Iran disrupts global oil routes.  Larijani dismissed Trump’s warnings as empty threats and said Iran would not be intimidated.

title: Bill Maher blasts the New York Times for ignoring Iranians celebrating Khamenei's death
description: Comedian Bill Maher has ripped The New York Times (NYT) for its coverage of the death of Iran’s former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.  Maher’s comments came during a segment on his ‘Club Random’ podcast, featuring neuroscientist Sam Harris.  The comedian pointed out that the New York Times’ headline following the US-led strikes was about the US service members who died.  The NYT’s article was accompanied by a photo of Iranian’s mourning the death of Khamenei.  “As we’re talking now, the Iran [war], the new Iran war, and The New York Times headline was, you know, ‘U.S. troops die’ – that was like what they led with. Now, that is a big part of the story, that troops have died, I mean, it’s kind of a bigger story than that,” Maher said.  “But then, in a country where I’ve read 80-90% of the people are thrilled that the Ayatollah is gone. What picture did they put? Picture of people mourning the Ayatollah.”
